title: High-energy astronomy
content: High energy astronomy is the study of astronomical objects that release electromagnetic radiation of highly energetic wavelength s. It includes X-ray astronomy, gamma-ray astronomy, and extreme UV astronomy, as well as studies of neutrino s and cosmic ray s. The physical study of these phenomena is referred to as high-energy astrophysics. Astronomical objects commonly studied in this field may include black hole s, neutron star s, active galactic nuclei, supernova e, kilonova e, supernova remnant s, and gamma ray burst s. Missions.
